hook: I was training to be a counsellor when I found out something that shocked me.
payload: The BetterHelp pay gap + the bench pivot to Faresay.
cta: I built the thing I want to use myself. If you're a therapist, come have a look.
notes: Cut from the founder-origin video. The keystone.

hook: A therapy app promised to keep your secrets. Then it got fined $7.8 million.
payload: FTC 2023 fine + ban for sharing mental-health data with Facebook.
cta: Your data should stay yours. That's the whole idea behind Faresay.
notes: Strongest, fully-sourced fact. Can show real FTC headline on screen.

hook: You pay for therapy. Want to know how little your therapist might actually keep?
cta: There's a fairer way to do this.
notes: Phrase as a GAP, never a fixed %.

hook: 'Therapists charge too much.' Do they, though?
payload: It's not the therapist taking the money — it's the platform in the middle.
cta: Take out the middleman, and it's fair for everyone. That's Faresay.

hook: A therapist ran her first session on Faresay this week.
payload: Booked, video, paid, money in 2 days, from a phone. No commission.
cta: That's it. That's the whole pitch.
notes: Use the live product screen-capture. Update once you have a real first therapist.

hook: A therapy platform brings in around a billion dollars a year. For providing a platform.
payload: The revenue scale of the incumbents versus who does the actual work — contrasted with Faresay's no-commission model.
cta: The work is the therapist's. The money should follow the work.
notes: Say REVENUE not profit (Teladoc posted losses). Contrast, not attack.

hook: On most therapy platforms, the clients aren't yours. They're the platform's.
payload: Client ownership / no lock-in. On Faresay the therapist keeps their own client relationships and can leave with them.
cta: On Faresay, your clients are yours. You keep them.
notes: Autonomy angle for therapists. No numbers needed — just the principle.

hook: Who actually owns your therapy notes?
payload: Data ownership/control. Ties the FTC data-sharing case to the principle that records and data stay with the therapist and client on Faresay.
cta: Your records, your clients, your data. That's the idea.
notes: FTC fact is the proof; don't claim criminal conviction (it was a settlement).

hook: The whole plan costs less than one session.
payload: Faresay pricing: free to start, optional monthly plan that costs less than a single session, no commission.
cta: Free to start. The optional plan costs less than a single session.
notes: Never quote a commission %. Pricing as a quiet fact, not a sales pitch.

hook: Online therapy can run a few hundred a month. People say it's pricey. It doesn't have to be.
payload: Use the client-cost figure softly as colour, then pivot to the fairer model.
cta: Fair for the therapist usually means fair for the client too.
notes: client_cost is ANECDOTAL_SOFT — keep it as colour ('people say'), never a hard headline stat.

hook: On a big platform, you don't set your price. They do.
payload: Pricing autonomy — Faresay therapists set their own prices and keep what they charge.
cta: Set your own prices. Keep what you charge.
notes: Frame as autonomy, not a dig at a named company.

hook: It promised to keep your secrets. Then it didn't.
payload: The trust-broken angle on the FTC case — a therapy app sharing sensitive data after promising not to.
cta: Trust is the product. We don't sell your data.
notes: Settlement, not conviction. Let the verified fact carry the anger.

hook: I found out how the big therapy apps really work. So I rebuilt it.
payload: Build-in-public origin variant — peer/trainee who could code and made the fair alternative.
cta: I built the thing I want to use myself.
notes: SCOPE: 'training to be a counsellor', never 'qualified therapist'. Build-in-public tone.

hook: Same video call. Same therapist. Very different split.
payload: Direct contrast: identical service, the difference is where the money goes — incumbent vs Faresay.
cta: There's a fairer way to do this.
notes: Frame pay as a GAP, never a fixed %. Strong split-screen visual.

hook: In the UK, your therapy notes are 'special category' data — the most protected kind there is.
payload: UK-framed trust angle: the law treats mental-health data as special category; that's the standard Faresay is built to.
cta: Your records stay with you and your therapist. That's the idea.
notes: State the law, not a Faresay certification. UK answer to the US FTC angle.

hook: Private therapy in the UK runs about fifty to ninety pounds a session. People feel that.
payload: UK cost as colour, then the fairer-model pivot — fair for the therapist tends to mean fair for the client.
cta: Fair for the therapist usually means fairer for the client too.
notes: Cost varies by region/modality — keep it a typical range, never a fixed price. Faresay doesn't set client prices.

hook: In England, the longest waits for mental-health care now stretch close to two years.
payload: Scale of unmet need: NHS waits are long, demand is huge, independent therapists carry a lot of it — Faresay helps them run fairly.
cta: Independent therapists carry a lot of this. Faresay helps them do it fairly.
notes: LONGEST waits, not typical. NEVER position Faresay as an NHS replacement or a crisis service.

hook: Around one in four of us will have a mental-health problem this year.
payload: Prevalence as context for why accessible, fair therapy matters — no outcome claims.
cta: Therapy should be easy to give and easy to get. That's the aim.
notes: Context only — no clinical-outcome claims. Don't imply Faresay treats or cures.
